name: Gmail API
description: Send transactional emails via Gmail API with OAuth 2.0 authentication
author: Spwig

oauth_credentials.label: OAuth Credentials
oauth_credentials.help_text: OAuth 2.0 credentials from Google Cloud Console

features: No additional cost (uses your Gmail account)

notes: Free with any Gmail or Google Workspace account

dns_requirements.dkim_info: DKIM is configured in Google Workspace Admin Console under Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Authenticate email
notes: Gmail API sending uses your Gmail/Google Workspace account
SPF record should include Google's servers
DKIM is configured separately in Google Workspace Admin Console
Standard Gmail accounts may not have DKIM configuration access
Google Workspace accounts have full DKIM control

changes: Fixed DMARC recommendation to use relaxed alignment (adkim=r; aspf=r) for multi-provider compatibility
Normalized spf_include value format (removed include: prefix for consistency)
Converted setup_instructions.html and dns_requirements.html to scoped HTML fragments
Eliminated standalone HTML documents with DOCTYPE and global CSS that caused style bleeding
All CSS now scoped under .setup-instructions via provider-wizard.css
Added i18n support with Django {% trans %} template tags
Replaced inline scripts with data-action attributes for CSP compliance
Tab switching uses si-tab/si-tab-content pattern to avoid class conflicts
Copy-to-clipboard buttons use data-action='copy-to-clipboard' pattern
Updated setup_instructions.html to use CSS variables for admin theme support
Updated dns_requirements.html to use CSS variables for theme support
Setup instructions now adapt properly to light/dark themes
Fixed CSS class name conflicts - renamed .step to .gmail-setup-step
Fixed CSS class name conflicts - renamed .steps to .gmail-dns-steps
All colors now use fallback values for maximum compatibility
Fixed SVG logo display issue by adding explicit width and height attributes
Logo now displays correctly on provider browse page
Initial release
OAuth 2.0 authentication with token refresh
Send emails via Gmail API
HTML and plain text email support
Attachment support (files up to 25MB)
Inline image support with Content-ID
CC and BCC recipient support
Custom headers support
Encrypted credential storage (Fernet encryption)
Comprehensive error handling (401, 403, 429, timeout)
Health check via Gmail API profile endpoint
Full unit and integration test coverage
